gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the OpenAI-compatible ownership inversion and found one P2 compatibility issue: legacy callers patching restaurant_kv_serving.openai_compatible.urlopen would no longer intercept HTTP calls. The issue was fixed by adding a document-owned _active_urlopen() resolver that honors patched legacy urlopen before the document _urlopen hook, with regression coverage for both hooks. Re-review found no issues.

issues.resolution: Added _active_urlopen() in document_kv_cache.openai_compatible and restored legacy urlopen monkeypatch coverage while preserving the new document hook.
issues.summary: Legacy urlopen monkeypatch compatibility was broken by moving the engine implementation into the document module.

scope: document and legacy package README migration notes
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_openai_compatible.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
repository secret-pattern scan over README.md CONTRIBUTING.md pyproject.toml src tests databricks pr-evidence .github; only intentional fake LangSmith fixtures were reported
what_changed: Moved the OpenAI-compatible completion engine implementation into document_kv_cache.openai_compatible.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.openai_compatible into a compatibility wrapper over the document module.
Kept the public document __all__ curated while preserving the legacy wrapper's broader historical import surface.
Preserved legacy urlopen monkeypatch behavior through a migration resolver and added regression tests.
Updated package READMEs and public-package tests to record the new implementation boundary.
why: The generalized package should own serving-platform benchmark integration directly because it is part of the public V1 workflow for arbitrary documents and external vLLM/SGLang OpenAI-compatible servers. This is a behavior-preserving migration step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
